title: Jobin
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Jobin.png]] |name=Jobin |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]"[[Entrenched]]" depicts Jobin's [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{StoryCite|story=Entrenched|book=Star Wars: Visionaries}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Mothma family/Legends|Mothma family]] |parents=*[[Jobin's father]] *[[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] |partners= |siblings=[[Lieda Mothma]]{{InsiderCite|82|Ask Lobot|Ask the Master}} |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance Military]] ***[[Wilderness Fighters]] ****[[4th Squad]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Me, I know when death comes—I can hold my head up high—stare death in the face—smile—And know that it's okay because I'm ''proud'' of the choices I've made in my life. Knowing that I've been a part of something that's ''made a difference'' to an entire galaxy. And when I die, I'll know my life will have had meaning.|Jobin, in his message to his mother, Mon Mothma|}} '''Jobin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] in the [[Alliance Military/Legends|military]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. He was also the [[Reproduction/Legends|son]] of [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], one of the founders of the Alliance, and was [[Mothma family/Legends|brother]] to [[Lieda Mothma]]. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in which the Alliance fought against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Jobin was stationed at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. He helped establish the perimeter defenses of the base and was assigned as leader of the [[4th Squad]]. In 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], knowing that the Empire had found Echo Base and was about to [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|attack]], Jobin began recording a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] message for his [[Parent/Legends|mother]], which he finished just as the first Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|walkers]] approached. During the battle, Jobin's attention was called to [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial forces]] proceeding to the base's [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|ion cannon]], which was essential to allow a safe evacuation. He led a group of [[Rebel trooper/Legends|troopers]], including [[Grond (Rebel)|Grond]], [[Sora]], [[Roj]], and another [[Unidentified Rebel trooper (Hoth)|trooper]], to retake the [[Ion cannon/Legends|cannon]]. While [[Death/Legends|losing]] two comrades, Jobin was successful in defeating the occupying [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]] and activating the ion [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]] to save one of the last evacuating [[Transport/Legends|transports]]. Jobin and his remaining companions then tried to reach a transport but were intercepted by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and several snowtroopers. Jobin was injured as the group tried to flee, and he rejected his comrade's attempt to help, instead passing on his recorded message and trying to hold off Vader. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Telekinesis/Legends|levitated]] Jobin with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and demanded the location of the Rebel [[Haven (rendezvous point)|rendezvous point]]. Jobin refused and spat in Vader's face, prompting his execution. The corporal's message and news of his death did not reach his mother until almost a [[4 ABY/Legends|year later]], shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. ==Biography== ===Early life and Hoth deployment=== {{Quote|My men and I spent long, arduous hours setting up the perimeter defenses, doing what we could in case we were found here.|Jobin|}} [[File:MonMothma-RotJAVA.png|thumb|right|250px|Jobin was the son of Mon Mothma (pictured)]] Jobin was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Mothma family/Legends|Mothma family]], the [[Reproduction/Legends|son]] of the Human [[Chandrilan/Legends|Chandrilan]] [[Senator/Legends|senator]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and a [[Jobin's father|male]] individual. He also had a sister named [[Lieda Mothma]] and was the grandson of [[Tanis Mothma]], [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of [[Hanna City/Legends|Hanna City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]],''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' and her [[Mon Mothma's father/Legends|spouse]], a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Arbiter-general/Legends|arbiter-general]].{{DB|character|monmothma|Mothma, Mon}} Jobin became a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], an organization his [[Parent/Legends|mother]] had founded to fight against the reigning [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Jobin had risen to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] in the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance Military]] and was stationed as part of the ground forces{{CSWECite|II|169|Jobin, Corporal}} at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. In spite of being the youngest [[Non-commissioned officer/Legends|noncommissioned officer]] in his [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]], Jobin was reassigned by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]] to move from his old unit to be leader of the [[4th Squad]]. Jobin found the adjustment difficult but felt his new companions, many of whom were veterans of battles such as [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Yavin]] and [[Battle of Daluuj|Daluuj]], were welcoming and encouraging to the Alliance's newer members. Jobin's [[Squad/Legends|squad]] spent a significant period of [[Time/Legends|time]] establishing the perimeter defenses of Echo Base. In 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] found and destroyed an Imperial [[Viper probe droid]] near the base, leading the Rebels to anticipate an [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|attack]] and commence evacuation procedures.{{Film|V}} [[File:Jobin_drinks.png|thumb|left|250px|Jobin shortly before the Battle of Hoth]] While waiting in the trenches at [[Echo Station 5-7/Legends|Echo Station 5-7]] in preparation, Jobin began recording a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] to his mother, despite his battalion [[Commanding officer/Legends|commander's]] orders against communicating with family. Jobin quietly ignored the orders, believing that he did not have time to wait any longer. He recounted his experiences with his squad and described the conditions on Hoth. He kept the planet's identity secret and only referred to the cold [[Temperature/Legends|temperature]], which had prevented their [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] from operating without modifications and required the domestication of the local [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]] for exploration. In his recording, Jobin acknowledged Solo's encounter with the [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] and that he felt prepared for the approaching [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]. He also commented on his beliefs about himself and his role, such as his fear of losing himself in war and his confidence that he had made a difference by fighting in the Rebel Alliance. As Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] approached [[Echo Station 3-T-8/Legends|Echo Station 3-T-8]], Jobin closed out his message by sending his love and best wishes to his mother, his father, and Lieda. ===The Battle of Hoth=== {{Quote|Grond, take Sora and circle around to the entrance. Wait for my signal then go in hard!''"
"''Roger that, corporal!|Jobin gives orders to Grond during the Battle of Hoth|}} The [[Troop/Legends|troops]] at Echo Station 5-7 came under attack from the Imperial [[AT-series/Legends|walkers]], prompting Jobin to shout into his [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] for air support, which immediately arrived in the form of modified [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeders]]. He called for a [[corpsman]] to assist a wounded [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], after which one of his companions pointed out an Imperial [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]] and several [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]] approaching Echo Base's [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|ion cannon]], which was essential to disable the encroaching Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and allowing the Rebel [[Transport/Legends|transports]] to safely evacuate. The corporal tried to contact the cannon's control center via comlink, but it had already been captured by snowtroopers. Jobin ordered four other [[Rebel trooper/Legends|troopers]]; [[Roj]], [[Sora]], [[Grond (Rebel)|Grond]], and another [[Unidentified Rebel trooper (Hoth)|trooper]]; to follow him, while also instructing the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] [[Jielu]] to remain to protect the south entrance. As they neared the [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]], Jobin ordered Grond and Sora to circle around Echo Base's entrance and wait for his signal to attack. The corporal, Roj, and the other trooper came under fire from the AT-ST but were able to take cover, leading the [[Walker/Legends|walker]] to target Grond and Sora and take its attention away from Jobin. This allowed the corporal to throw an explosive package at the AT-ST and bring it down. [[File:Jobin_icicle.png|thumb|right|300px|Jobin escapes the clutches of a snowtrooper.]] Jobin and his team of five crept in to retake the control center from the occupying snowtroopers, knowing that any further transports would be unable to escape without an active ion [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]]. The corporal gave the order to fire on the snowtroopers from the control center's entrance, hitting several of them, although one of the Rebel troopers was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by returning fire. Another grabbed a comlink to announce that Imperial troops had entered the base and was gunned down in the process. Jobin lunged for the ion cannon's control panel and was caught in a chokehold by one of the snowtroopers. In order to free himself, Jobin snapped a nearby icicle from the ground and shoved it back into the snowtrooper's [[Eye/Legends|eye]], freeing himself to activate the ion cannon and disable an [[Rebel Dream|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]. His actions allowed some of the last Rebel transports to escape Hoth. While Jobin looked in horror at one of his deceased comrades, one of the two survivors informed him that they had activated the cannon's [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destruct sequence]] and that the group needed to flee. Jobin began moving out, knowing that they could potentially join one of the final evacuating transports in [[Hangar Bay 7]]. ===Death=== {{Quote|Save your mercy for someone else.|Jobin's last words, spoken to Darth Vader|}} [[File:Jobin_death.png|thumb|left|350px|Darth Vader used the Force to execute Jobin]] As the three troopers proceeded, they encountered several snowtroopers and the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who had just witnessed the takeoff of the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Vader ordered his soldiers to capture the rebels, who evaded their blasterfire as Jobin ordered them to get to [[Hangar Ten]]. The [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] contained a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]], but just as the rebels neared it, Jobin was hit by blasterfire on his left side. Roj yelled and tried to run back for the corporal, but Jobin ordered them to escape, passing over his pre-recorded hologram so that his mother could receive it. While the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] escaped, Jobin pulled out a [[Relby-k23 blaster pistol]] to fire at the snowtroopers and give his companions more time to escape. He succeeded at killing one of them while Vader blocked his next shots with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] until Jobin's blaster was empty. [[Force power|Using]] the force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|lift]] Jobin into the air, Vader demanded that he reveal the location of the [[Haven (rendezvous point)|Rebel rendezvous point]], promising mercy if he did so. Jobin rejected the offer and spat in Vader's face, telling him to use his mercy elsewhere. In response, Vader telekinetically snapped Jobin's neck. Mon Mothma was unaware of Jobin's fate until nearly a [[4 ABY/Legends|year later]], when she received Jobin's hologram while aboard the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']]. She pondered why the message had taken so long to reach her, and just as it finished playing, the Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] entered Mothma's quarters to inform her that the Alliance members aboard the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] were ready for her briefing. Mothma asked if there had been any news about Jobin, and Madine confirmed that he had been killed at the Battle of Hoth. The subsequent briefing set the stage for the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which resulted in the deaths of Vader and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Funny thing is, my biggest sense of dread doesn't circle around my physical death—but around the death of my ''soul''. I want so badly to be the same man I was when I left you and the others—but I fear with what I've seen… with what I'll do… you have to do unspeakable things to survive.|Jobin|}} [[File:Jobin_destroys_AT-ST.png|thumb|right|350px|Jobin destroys an AT-ST during the Battle of Hoth]] Jobin was loyal to the Alliance to Restore the Republic and believed in the value of his actions. He chose to ignore his commander's orders in order to send a recording to his mother, correctly anticipating that he would not have another opportunity. He observed that the military veterans in his squad were afraid of the future and death in spite of their tough exteriors. Jobin hoped that his squad's combat abilities would be enough to ensure that they could fight instinctually and without the liability of thinking. In his recording to his mother, he also expressed concerns about losing his morality, which he feared more than his physical death, and recognized that he would need to commit atrocities and adapt to survive, but he also focused on the greater good he wanted to achieve rather than his personal sacrifices, including those he could not keep alive. Jobin reflected that many beings perceived their lives as a series of choices that they would look back on and evaluate, but was confident that when his death came that he would hold his head high and smile in pride at his decisions, particularly his role in the Rebel Alliance. He felt that he had given his life purpose and meaning in trying to make a difference for [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. After being shot by snowtroopers, Jobin ordered his comrades to leave him behind so that they could escape, only passing on his message for his mother. He exhibited open defiance towards [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, who had no time for his delay and killed him quickly. Jobin had [[Color/Legends|light]] skin, black eyes, and black [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|I pray we've gotten to the point where our actions are more reflex than thought.|Jobin, to his mother|}} Jobin was trained as a Rebel soldier and became a corporal in the [[Alliance Army/Legends|Alliance Army]]. During the Battle of Hoth, he threw an explosive package onto an AT-ST, destroying it. While being put into a chokehold by a snowtrooper, Jobin was resourceful enough to use an icicle to stab his assailant in the eye and free himself. ==Equipment== {{Quote|Give… Give this to my mother!|Jobin, passing his recording onto one of his Rebel comrades|}} On Hoth, Jobin wore [[Alliance Cold Weather armor]], consisting of a tan [[Clothing/Legends|vest]], thick white coverings on his [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], and a cap with adjustable [[Anti-glare goggles/Legends|goggles]]. He wore a red sash around his headgear and carried a personal hologram with which he could record messages. At one point, shortly before the Battle of Hoth commenced, Jobin drank a warm beverage from a cup. In battle, he wielded an [[A295 blaster rifle/Legends|A295 blaster rifle]], a Relby-k23 [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], and an explosive package. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Swvis1cover.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jobin was introduced by the comic "Entrenched," which was collected in the anthology ''Star Wars Visionaries'' (cover pictured).]] Jobin was created for the short comic story "[[Entrenched]]," created for the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''. The comic was written by [[Alex Jaeger]] and [[M. Zachary Sherman]], and also illustrated by Jaeger. Jobin was first indirectly mentioned in a preview of ''Visionaries'' published on [[StarWars.com]] on [[March 15]], [[2005]], prior to the publication of the graphic novel by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005.{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20050315.html|text=Inside ''Star Wars: Visionaries''|date=2005-03-15|quote=On April 2nd, Dark Horse Comics will release ''Star Wars: Visionaries'', a unique graphic novel collecting tales illustrated by the concept artists who helped envision the worlds, characters and story of Episode III. (...) '''Entrenched by Alex Jaeger with M. Zachary Sherman''' The trenches of Hoth are the setting for this hard-hitting military action, as we witness the relentless Imperial attack from a young Rebel soldier's point-of-view.}} Author [[Pablo Hidalgo]] mentioned Jobin in response to a fan question about Lieda Mothma in the "[[Ask Lobot|Ask the Master]]" section of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 82|82nd issue]], which began shipping to subscribers on [[May 17]], 2005.{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20050506.html|text=The Dark Side Shines in ''Star Wars Insider'' #82|date=2005-05-06|quote=''Star Wars Insider'' #82 starts shipping to subscribers on May 17th, and begins shipping to newsstands on May 30th. }} At the time, Hidalgo acknowledged Jobin's sibling relation to Lieda but acknowledged that there was no indication as to the identity of their father or if either of them shared their mother's surname, although Lieda's full name was later [[canon]]ically confirmed within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity by Jobin's entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[2008]]. The Encyclopedia also stated that Jobin reported on the presence of Imperial forces within Echo Base, however, in "Entrenched," a different trooper announces that the base has been penetrated before being killed by snowtroopers. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jobin}} *{{StoryCite|story=Entrenched|book=Star Wars: Visionaries}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20050315.html|text=Inside ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}} {{1stm}} {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}} {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|82|Star Wars Q&A|Ask the Master}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{FactFile2014|45|ANT 1-4|DF.9 Anti-infantry battery}} {{Qo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jobin |pl=Jobin Mothma }} [[Category:Alliance Army officers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mothma family]]
